free_text: Sn-120(p,a)In-117 and Sb-121(p,a)Sn-118 reactions:
Identification of the In-117 and Sn-118 homologous
states.
MP Tandem accelerator of the
University and Technical University of Munich
The Stern-Gerlach source for intense, bright beams of
negatively polarized hydrogen ions.
The 41 mug/cm**2 thick Sn-120 and 56 mug/cm**2 thick
Sb-121 isotopically enriched (99.6 and 99.53%
respectively) targets were evaporated onto an
9 mug/cm**2 carbon backing.
The reaction products were momentum
separated by the Q3D magnetic spectrograph.
Outgoing alpha particles were identified by
the new proportional counter with cathode-strip readout
focal plane, designed to detect light ions such as p, d
for the Q3D with a position resolution of better than
0.1 mm, good particle identification and high count
rate.
The particles were stopped in a 7 mm thick
plastic scintillator (NE-104) which ensured both dE-E
particle identification and good position resolution.
.DWBA analysis.
.The uncertainty is reported by authors in
tables.
